citations.narrative:
A documented contingency plan for the facility was not available for review. Without adequate contingency planning, the
animals at the facility may not be provided with appropriate protection and care in the event of an emergency.
The licensee must develop, document, and follow an appropriate contingency plan [in accordance with 2.134(a)(1) to (4)]
to provide for the humane handling, treatment, transportation, housing, and care of their animals in the event of an
emergency or disaster (one which could reasonably be anticipated and expected to be detrimental to the good health and
well-being of the animals in their possession). The plan must be available for review during APHIS inspections. The
facility must correct this issue.
********Correct by May 15, 2026
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the facility representative.
n

narrative:
2.134(a) 
   
Contingency planning. 
A documented contingency plan for the facility was not available for review. Without adequate contingency planning, the 
animals at the facility may not be provided with appropriate protection and care in the event of an emergency. 
 
The licensee must develop, document, and follow an appropriate contingency plan [in accordance with 2.134(a)(1) to (4)] 
to provide for the humane handling, treatment, transportation, housing, and care of their animals in the event of an 
emergency or disaster (one which could reasonably be anticipated and expected to be detrimental to the good health and 
well-being of the animals in their possession). The plan must be available for review during APHIS inspections. The 
facility must correct this issue. 
********Correct by May 15, 2026 
 
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the facility representative.
